briefing.headline: Rig up a Carolina Rig — aggressive spawn approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Carolina Rig is a strong choice for spawn with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Rigs a curly-tail worm (cut paddle tail) on a Carolina rig with 3/4-oz egg sinker, 22-inch fluorocarbon leader (range 18-26 inches), and 3/0 worm hook; retrieves with standard pull-and-drag devoid of deadstick pauses.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Rigs a curly-tail worm (cut paddle tail) on a Carolina rig with 3/4-oz egg sinker, 22-inch fluorocarbon leader (range 18-26 inches), and 3/0 worm hook; retrieves with standard pull-and-drag d
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Yamamoto pro Yelas: 'Right around the spawn and after, the fish seem to prefer a smaller bait with less action' — downsize from the Cowboy during spawn and post-spawn
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Yamamoto pro Fukae: The Free Rig is a cross between a Texas Rig and Carolina Rig — the line runs freely through the weight's eye so the weight falls first and the soft plastic flutters down behind it with an unpredictable slow fall; use a 'lift an...

KVD: 'Don't assume you must finesse spawning bass. At the 2011 Classic, 4 of top 7 finishers power fished spinnerbaits and crankbaits on the same 150-yard spawning flat. Key conditions: hard bottom flat with stumps + grass, 10-degree water temp ri...
Wheeler: Carolina rig setup uses 7.5-foot medium-heavy rod, 7.0:1 reel, 17-lb fluorocarbon mainline to barrel swivel, then 14-lb fluorocarbon leader of 22 inches (18-26 inch range).
Hackney: 'At Stick Marsh with no underwater vegetation, fish were spawning around willow trees and cane on the shoreline - they just wanted to have something to spawn around.'

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal spawning bass are active on beds in 2-5ft. Falling pressure often triggers new waves of fish to move onto beds. Target hard-bottom spawning flats, secondary pockets, and protected coves.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If bites dry up, try shaking in place for 10-15 seconds before moving — patience pays with plastics
